title: Mistral’s CEO warns closed AI can expose business processes
friendlyTitle: Mistral says companies should be careful with closed AI tools
summary: The Decoder covers Mistral CEO Arthur Mensch’s argument that companies relying on closed AI models risk giving model providers visibility into sensitive business processes. The article notes the argument is relevant but also commercially aligned with Mistral’s open and sovereignty positioning.
friendlySummary: Mistral’s CEO argues that closed AI providers may learn too much about how a business works. The article also notes that Mistral benefits from making this argument.
whyItMatters: As AI moves from chat into workflows, the data-governance question gets sharper: who sees prompts, files, decisions, and process patterns? Businesses should compare convenience against control, auditability, and the strategic value of internal knowledge.
friendlyWhyItMatters: When a company uses AI for sensitive work, it should ask where its data goes and who can see it. Convenience is useful, but control matters too.
